problem_statement: Short links currently live forever; we need opt-in expiry. POST /api/links should accept an optional ttlSeconds field: when present it must be a positive integer, otherwise the request fails with 400 and body {"error": "invalid_ttl"}. Every link JSON view returned by create, get-by-slug and list must include an expiresAt field, an ISO-8601 timestamp equal to the creation time plus ttlSeconds, or null for links created without a ttl. Redirecting through GET /r/:slug for a link whose expiry has been reached (current time >= expiresAt, boundary inclusive) must answer 410 with {"error": "link_expired"} and must NOT record a click. Metadata reads via GET /api/links/:slug keep returning 200 for expired links so owners can inspect them. All time handling must flow from the app's injected clock.
notes: RE-LABELED 2026-07-06 from k=5 calibration (cheap=haiku 5/5, frontier=sonnet 5/5): no routing signal at authored difficulty — measured reality is cheap-solvable. authored_difficulty preserved.
